title: MCP Trust & Rug-Pull Monitor input
description: Defensive, metadata-only monitoring for public or explicitly authorized MCP endpoints. Every run requires an authorization attestation.

servers.title: Authorized MCP servers
servers.description: One to 25 public MCP metadata endpoints that you own, are authorized to inspect, or that intentionally expose public metadata. Disabled entries count toward the 25-entry limit.

name.title: Server name
name.description: Stable human-readable server name used with the endpoint URL to derive baseline keys.

url.title: Metadata endpoint URL
url.description: Absolute HTTP(S) MCP metadata URL without URL credentials, fragments, or credential-shaped query parameters. HTTPS is required unless allowHttp is explicitly enabled.

transport.title: Transport
transport.description: Metadata discovery transport. auto tries bounded hosted transports; http_sse is recognized but intentionally returns unsupported_transport in this release.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: When false, keep this configured server in the input without inspecting it during this run.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Optional labels for organizing this server. Up to 20 non-empty labels of at most 128 characters each are accepted.

headers.title: Secret request headers
headers.description: Optional authorized request headers. The containing servers array is encrypted as secret Actor input, covering this entire object. Values must be non-empty, contain no line breaks, and be at most 4096 characters; total header names and values are limited to 8192 bytes at runtime. Host, connection, length, transfer, upgrade, proxy, TE, and trailer headers are rejected. Never put secrets in URLs, tags, or names.

authorizedUseConfirmed.title: Authorization attestation
authorizedUseConfirmed.description: Required. Set true only when every target intentionally exposes public metadata, belongs to you, or you have explicit permission to inspect it. False is rejected by the Actor.

baselineKeyValueStoreId.title: Persistent baseline key-value store
baselineKeyValueStoreId.description: Optional persistent Key-Value Store for trusted and candidate baselines. Required together with a baseline Request Queue for non-dry-run initialization, update, or promotion. Reuse the same selected stores across scheduled runs.

baselineRequestQueueId.title: Persistent baseline lock queue
baselineRequestQueueId.description: Optional persistent Request Queue used only for atomic baseline mutation locks. Required together with the baseline Key-Value Store for non-dry-run initialization, update, or promotion.

baselineMode.title: Baseline mode
baselineMode.description: Controls trusted baseline persistence. The safe default compare_only never creates or updates a trusted baseline. Choose a mutating mode only after reviewing the endpoint and persistence behavior.

promoteCandidateBaseline.title: Promote matching candidate
promoteCandidateBaseline.description: When true, promote and consume a compatible stored candidate only if its endpoint identity, current snapshot hash, and exact trusted-parent hash (or explicit no-parent state) match the current state. Any mismatch returns promotion_mismatch and preserves both records.

minimumAlertSeverity.title: Minimum webhook severity
minimumAlertSeverity.description: Lowest report severity eligible for the optional webhook. Status-specific alert rules still apply, and webhook delivery occurs only after Dataset persistence succeeds.

webhookUrl.title: Alert webhook URL
webhookUrl.description: Optional public HTTPS webhook URL without URL credentials. Do not include tokens or other secrets in the URL.

checkVulnerabilities.title: Check OSV vulnerabilities
checkVulnerabilities.description: Query the public OSV API only for package identities and versions explicitly exposed by server metadata. Unavailable lookups are reported without fabricating matches.

checkTls.title: Inspect TLS metadata
checkTls.description: Inspect HTTPS certificate validity, hostname, expiry, and negotiated protocol metadata before the MCP metadata request.

includeRawNormalizedSnapshot.title: Include normalized snapshot
includeRawNormalizedSnapshot.description: Include the redacted canonical metadata snapshot in each Dataset report. Leave disabled unless downstream review requires the larger payload.

requestTimeoutSeconds.title: Request timeout
requestTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for each bounded network operation, from 1 to 120 seconds. A separate per-server wall-clock limit also applies.

maxRetries.title: Maximum retries
maxRetries.description: Maximum retry count for eligible metadata requests, from zero to four. Authentication and other terminal failures are not retried indefinitely.

concurrency.title: Server concurrency
concurrency.description: Number of enabled servers inspected concurrently, from one to ten.

dryRun.title: Dry run
dryRun.description: Inspect and compare without creating, updating, promoting, or replacing trusted or candidate baseline records.

allowHttp.title: Allow plain HTTP
allowHttp.description: Explicitly opt in to public plain-HTTP targets. Disabled by default because HTTP provides no transport confidentiality or certificate validation.

allowPrivateNetwork.title: Allow private networks
allowPrivateNetwork.description: Reserved compatibility field that must remain false. Private, loopback, link-local, metadata-service, local-file, Unix-socket, and stdio targets are unsupported by this hosted Actor.

maxResponseBytes.title: Maximum metadata response size
maxResponseBytes.description: Maximum accepted metadata response body size, from 16 KiB to 5 MiB. Oversized or incomplete responses never update a trusted baseline.
